title: State senator, city auditor: NY aid to cities isn’t keeping up (Your Letters)
summary: State Senator Christopher J. Ryan and City Auditor Alexander Marion advocate for a state commission to redesign New York's municipal aid formula (AIM), which has been frozen since 2012. Syracuse relies heavily on state aid as its second-largest revenue source, but cost inflation in salaries, benefits, and infrastructure has outpaced frozen funding, creating fiscal sustainability concerns across upstate cities.

political_signal.description: Advocacy from state senator (Chair of Cities 2 Committee) and city auditor for legislative action to establish commission redesigning municipal aid formula; framed as necessary intervention to prevent fiscal cliff and sustain city services.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts: $50 million (state infusion), $5 million (Syracuse portion), $13.5 million (unrelated opinion piece) — only first two relevant to article
AIM freeze occurred in 2012 under Gov. Cuomo; modest $50M infusion in prior year under Gov. Hochul represents partial thaw
Key CIO concerns: state aid dependency, infrastructure cost inflation, cybersecurity challenges, tax-exempt property growth (hospitals/universities)
Tag warning: Unknown tag 'advocacy' not in taxonomy
